_note: Text-capable local models pullable from the Ollama library. sizeGB = download/disk size at the default quant (usually Q4_K_M). kvKbPerTok = approximate KV-cache cost per token in KB at fp16, used to recommend a context window that fits the user's memory budget — it is an estimate derived from layer count x KV heads x head dim, not a measured value. ctxMax = the model's trained context limit. tags drive the filter chips in the Download tab.

_tagLegend.vision: Accepts images as well as text
_tagLegend.moe: Mixture-of-experts — fewer active params than total
_tagLegend.tiny: Runs on almost anything, including CPU-only
_tagLegend.abliterated: Refusal direction removed via orthogonalisation — answers where the base model would decline

models.name: SmolLM2 135M
models.blurb: About as small as a usable language model gets. Fine for autocomplete-grade text on a Raspberry Pi; don't expect reasoning.

models.name: SmolLM2 360M
models.blurb: Still tiny, noticeably more coherent than the 135M. Good for simple summarising and classification on weak hardware.

models.name: Qwen2.5 0.5B
models.blurb: Punches above its weight for the size and handles 32K context. A sensible floor model for old laptops.

models.name: TinyLlama 1.1B
models.blurb: The classic small Llama. Superseded by Llama 3.2 1B in almost every way, but it's fast and runs anywhere.

models.name: Qwen3 0.6B
models.blurb: Smallest Qwen3. Inherits the family's toggleable thinking mode, so it can reason step-by-step despite the size.

models.name: Llama 3.2 1B
models.blurb: Meta's smallest instruct model. Genuinely coherent for its size and takes a huge context. Good pick for CPU-only machines.

models.name: Qwen2.5 1.5B
models.blurb: Strong multilingual ability for a sub-1GB download. Reliable at short structured tasks.

models.name: Gemma 3 1B
models.blurb: Google's compact Gemma. Clean, well-behaved prose; weaker at code than the Qwen equivalents.

models.name: Qwen3 1.7B
models.blurb: The sweet spot in Qwen3's tiny tier. Thinking mode makes it surprisingly capable at multi-step questions.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 1.5B
models.blurb: A reasoning-distilled model small enough for a phone-class machine. Shows its working on maths and logic.

models.name: SmolLM2 1.7B
models.blurb: The largest SmolLM2. Trained on a heavily filtered corpus — clean output, modest world knowledge.

models.name: Moondream 1.8B
models.blurb: A tiny vision model that describes images and does OCR. Runs on hardware no other vision model will touch.

models.name: Gemma 2 2B
models.blurb: Well-liked small model with a distinctly polite, careful voice. Solid general chat under 2GB.

models.name: Qwen2.5 3B
models.blurb: Reliable tool-caller at a small size — a good agentic model for machines that can't hold 7B.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B
models.blurb: The best all-round model that still fits in 4GB. Fast, coherent, and handles long documents.

models.name: Phi-3 Mini
models.blurb: Microsoft's textbook-trained small model. Unusually strong at reasoning and code for under 3GB.

models.name: Qwen3 4B
models.blurb: Thinking mode in a 4B package. A strong default if 8B is too tight for your machine.

models.name: Gemma 3 4B
models.blurb: Reads images as well as text, at a size most laptops can handle. Good general-purpose multimodal starter.

models.name: DeepSeek-Coder 1.3B
models.blurb: A dedicated code model under 1GB. Useful for completion and boilerplate, not architecture.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 1.5B
models.blurb: Tiny coder with fill-in-the-middle support. Good for inline completion on modest hardware.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 3B
models.blurb: Noticeably better than the 1.5B at whole-function generation while staying under 2GB.

models.name: StarCoder2 3B
models.blurb: Trained on permissively-licensed source across 600+ languages. Strong at obscure languages.

models.name: CodeGemma 2B
models.blurb: Built for fast code infilling rather than chat. Pair it with a general model for explanations.

models.name: Llama 3.1 8B
models.blurb: The workhorse open model. Broad knowledge, dependable tool use, and a 128K context. A safe default at 8GB+.

models.name: Qwen2.5 7B
models.blurb: Balanced 7B that codes better than most of its peers. Excellent multilingual coverage.

models.name: Mistral 7B
models.blurb: The model that started the open-weights wave. Terse, fast, and still a fine general chat model.

models.name: Qwen3 8B
models.blurb: Strong agentic model with toggleable thinking. A good choice for tool-heavy work on a 12GB+ machine.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 7B
models.blurb: Reasoning-distilled from R1. Thinks visibly before answering — slower, but better on hard problems.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 8B
models.blurb: The Llama-based R1 distill. Slightly broader world knowledge than the 7B Qwen-based one.

models.name: Hermes 3 8B
models.blurb: Nous Research's Llama 3.1 tune, built for function calling and steerability. A solid agent backbone.

models.name: Dolphin 3 8B
models.blurb: Instruction-tuned with refusals largely trained out. Useful when a safety-tuned model won't engage with your task.

models.name: Gemma 2 9B
models.blurb: Excellent writing quality for its size. The 8K context is the main limitation.

models.name: GLM-4 9B
models.blurb: Zhipu's bilingual model. Very strong Chinese/English, competitive with Llama 3.1 8B in English.

models.name: OLMo 2 7B
models.blurb: Fully open — weights, data, and training code all published. The pick when reproducibility matters.

models.name: Falcon 3 7B
models.blurb: TII's third-generation Falcon. Competitive general model with strong maths for the tier.

models.name: Aya Expanse 8B
models.blurb: Cohere's multilingual specialist covering 23 languages properly rather than incidentally.

models.name: EXAONE 3.5 7.8B
models.blurb: LG's bilingual Korean/English model. Strong instruction-following, long-context capable.

models.name: Granite 3.1 Dense 8B
models.blurb: IBM's enterprise-focused model. Apache-2.0 licensed and tuned for RAG and tool use.

models.name: Solar 10.7B
models.blurb: Upstage's depth-scaled model — punched above its weight at release and still writes well.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 7B
models.blurb: The best small coding model available locally. Competitive with much larger models on real tasks.

models.name: DeepSeek-Coder 6.7B
models.blurb: Trained on 87% code. Excellent at completion and repository-level context for its size.

models.name: CodeGemma 7B
models.blurb: Google's code model. Good at Python and Java; the 8K context limits whole-file work.

models.name: StarCoder2 7B
models.blurb: Broad language coverage with clean licensing. A dependable completion engine.

models.name: OpenCoder 8B
models.blurb: Fully-open code model with published data pipeline. Strong on Python and JavaScript.

models.name: CodeQwen 1.5 7B
models.blurb: Long-context code model — 64K lets it hold several large files at once.

models.name: LLaVA 7B
models.blurb: The original open vision-language model. Describes images and answers questions about them.

models.name: LLaVA-Llama3 8B
models.blurb: LLaVA's vision head on a Llama 3 base — better language quality than the original LLaVA.

models.name: MiniCPM-V 8B
models.blurb: Strong OCR and document understanding. Better than LLaVA at reading text inside images.

models.name: Qwen2.5-VL 7B
models.blurb: Excellent all-round vision model — OCR, charts, UI screenshots, and spatial reasoning.

models.name: Llama 3.2 Vision 11B
models.blurb: Meta's multimodal model. Reliable image reasoning with a very large context window.

models.name: Mistral Nemo 12B
models.blurb: Mistral and NVIDIA's joint 12B. Great multilingual coverage and a genuinely usable 128K context.

models.name: Gemma 3 12B
models.blurb: The mid-tier Gemma 3. Handles images, writes well, and fits comfortably in 12GB of VRAM.

models.name: Phi-4 14B
models.blurb: Microsoft's synthetic-data model. Outperforms much larger models on maths and reasoning benchmarks.

models.name: Qwen2.5 14B
models.blurb: The value pick in the mid tier — near-32B quality at roughly half the memory.

models.name: Qwen3 14B
models.blurb: Qwen3's mid-size model with thinking mode. Excellent agentic behaviour if you have 16GB to spare.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 14B
models.blurb: The mid-tier R1 distill. A real step up in reasoning over the 7B/8B versions.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 14B
models.blurb: Strong enough for real refactoring work, not just completion. Great local coding assistant at 16GB.

models.name: DeepSeek-Coder-V2 Lite 16B
models.blurb: Mixture-of-experts coder — only 2.4B params active, so it runs fast while holding 16B of knowledge.

models.name: LLaVA 13B
models.blurb: The larger LLaVA. Better visual detail than the 7B, same short context.

models.name: Mistral Small 22B
models.blurb: Mistral's mid-weight flagship. Genuinely strong general model if you have 16GB+ of VRAM.

models.name: Codestral 22B
models.blurb: Mistral's dedicated code model covering 80+ languages. Excellent fill-in-the-middle.

models.name: Gemma 2 27B
models.blurb: Best-in-class writing quality among open models at release. Held back by the 8K context.

models.name: Gemma 3 27B
models.blurb: The flagship Gemma 3 — multimodal, 128K context, and excellent prose. Wants a 24GB card.

models.name: Qwen3 30B-A3B
models.blurb: Frontier reasoning with only ~3B params active — fast on a 24GB card while matching much larger dense models.

models.name: Qwen2.5 32B
models.blurb: Dense 32B with broad capability. A serious local assistant if you can hold 20GB.

models.name: Qwen3 32B
models.blurb: The dense Qwen3 flagship. Slower than the 30B-A3B MoE but stronger on knowledge-heavy tasks.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 32B
models.blurb: The strongest R1 distill that fits on a single 24GB card. Excellent at maths and proofs.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 32B
models.blurb: The best open coding model you can run on one consumer GPU. Rivals frontier cloud models on many tasks.

models.name: Command R 35B
models.blurb: Cohere's RAG-tuned model. Built for grounded generation with citations over your own documents.

models.name: Aya Expanse 32B
models.blurb: The large multilingual Aya. The best open option if you work across many languages.

models.name: Yi 34B
models.blurb: 01.AI's bilingual model. Strong English and Chinese, good long-context recall.

models.name: Code Llama 34B
models.blurb: Meta's large code model. Largely superseded by Qwen2.5-Coder but still solid on C and C++.

models.name: LLaVA 34B
models.blurb: The largest LLaVA. Best visual reasoning in the family; short context and heavy.

models.name: Mixtral 8x7B
models.blurb: The MoE that made sparse models mainstream. Fast for its knowledge, but wants 32GB.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B
models.blurb: Meta's strongest open instruct model — roughly 405B quality at 70B cost. Needs a 48GB card or dual GPUs.

models.name: Llama 3.1 70B
models.blurb: The previous 70B flagship. Still excellent; 3.3 is strictly better at the same size.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 70B
models.blurb: The largest R1 distill. Approaches frontier reasoning if you have the hardware to hold it.

models.name: Qwen2.5 72B
models.blurb: Qwen's dense flagship. One of the strongest open models overall, at serious hardware cost.

models.name: Nemotron 70B
models.blurb: NVIDIA's RLHF-tuned Llama 3.1. Tuned hard for helpfulness; scored exceptionally on human-preference evals.

models.name: Athene V2 72B
models.blurb: A Qwen2.5-72B tune targeting chat, code, and maths. Competitive with frontier cloud models.

models.name: Llama 3.2 Vision 90B
models.blurb: Meta's large multimodal model. Workstation-class hardware only.

models.name: Command R+ 104B
models.blurb: Cohere's flagship RAG model. Excellent grounded generation; needs 64GB+.

models.name: Mixtral 8x22B
models.blurb: The large Mixtral MoE. Strong and comparatively fast to run — if you can fit 80GB.

models.name: Qwen3 235B-A22B
models.blurb: Qwen3's frontier MoE. Genuinely competitive with closed models — and needs a multi-GPU server.

models.name: Llama 3.1 405B
models.blurb: Meta's largest open model. Datacentre hardware only — listed for completeness.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 671B
models.blurb: The full R1, not a distill. Frontier-grade reasoning; needs a rack, not a desk.

models.name: Gemma 3 270M
models.blurb: Google's smallest Gemma. Built for on-device classification and extraction rather than conversation.

models.name: Gemma 3n E2B
models.blurb: Engineered for phones and laptops — selective parameter loading keeps memory use far below the raw size.

models.name: Gemma 3n E4B
models.blurb: The larger on-device Gemma 3n. Good quality-per-megabyte on everyday hardware.

models.name: Phi-4 Mini
models.blurb: Microsoft's small Phi-4. Strong multilingual, maths, and reasoning for under 3GB, with tool calling.

models.name: Granite 4.1 3B
models.blurb: IBM's compact enterprise model. Apache-2.0, tuned for RAG and structured extraction.

models.name: Granite 4.1 30B
models.blurb: The large Granite 4.1. Enterprise-focused with strong multilingual and long-context handling.

models.name: Falcon 3 1B
models.blurb: The smallest Falcon 3. Decent maths and science for the size.

models.name: Falcon 3 10B
models.blurb: TII's largest sub-10B-class model. Targets science, maths, and code specifically.

models.name: QwQ 32B
models.blurb: Qwen's dedicated reasoning model. Thinks at length before answering — excellent on maths and logic puzzles.

models.name: OpenThinker 7B
models.blurb: Fully open reasoning model distilled from DeepSeek-R1. Published data and recipe.

models.name: OpenThinker 32B
models.blurb: The large fully-open reasoner. Competitive with R1's own distills.

models.name: DeepScaleR 1.5B
models.blurb: A 1.5B that beats o1-preview on maths benchmarks. Narrow but remarkable at what it does.

models.name: Granite Code 3B
models.blurb: IBM's small code model, trained on cleanly-licensed source across 116 languages.

models.name: Granite Code 34B
models.blurb: The large Granite Code. Enterprise-friendly licensing for commercial codegen.

models.name: StarCoder2 15B
models.blurb: The largest StarCoder2. Best-in-family repository-level completion.

models.name: DeepSeek-Coder 33B
models.blurb: The large first-gen DeepSeek coder. Still strong on systems languages.

models.name: Code Llama 7B
models.blurb: Meta's small code model. Largely superseded, but a light option for completion.

models.name: Code Llama 13B
models.blurb: The mid-size Code Llama. Reasonable infilling; newer Qwen coders beat it.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 0.5B
models.blurb: Absolute smallest usable coder. Autocomplete only, but runs on anything.

models.name: Hermes 3 3B
models.blurb: The small Hermes 3. Good function calling for its size — a light agent backbone.

models.name: Hermes 3 70B
models.blurb: Nous Research's large flagship. Excellent steerability and agentic behaviour.

models.name: Mistral Small 3.2 24B
models.blurb: The refreshed Mistral Small — now multimodal, with better instruction-following than 3.1.

models.name: Magistral 24B
models.blurb: Mistral's reasoning model. Shows transparent multi-step working, tuned for European languages.

models.name: Devstral 24B
models.blurb: Mistral's agentic coding model — built to drive tools and edit real repositories, not just complete lines.

models.name: gpt-oss 20B
models.blurb: OpenAI's open-weight MoE. Only ~3.6B params active, so it runs fast on a 16GB card with configurable reasoning effort.

models.name: gpt-oss 120B
models.blurb: The large open-weight OpenAI MoE. Near-frontier reasoning; wants 80GB but is sparse enough to run well when it fits.

models.name: Llama 4 Scout
models.blurb: Meta's multimodal MoE with a 10M-token context ceiling. Only 17B active, so it's quicker than the size suggests.

models.name: Llama 4 Maverick
models.blurb: The large Llama 4. Frontier multimodal quality at 17B active params — but 245GB of weights to hold.

models.name: Qwen3.5 0.8B
models.blurb: The smallest Qwen3.5. Multimodal-capable family with thinking mode, at a size that runs anywhere.

models.name: Qwen3.5 9B
models.blurb: The everyday Qwen3.5. Vision, thinking, and tool use in a package that fits a 12GB card comfortably.

models.name: Qwen3.5 32B
models.blurb: The dense Qwen3.5 flagship for single-GPU rigs. Strong across vision, reasoning, and agentic work.

models.name: Qwen3.5 122B
models.blurb: The largest local Qwen3.5. Workstation-class, but genuinely frontier-adjacent.

models.name: Qwen3.6 27B
models.blurb: Widely rated the best all-round local model of 2026. Improved agentic coding and better thinking preservation across turns.

models.name: Qwen3.6 35B
models.blurb: The larger Qwen3.6. A step up on hard agentic tasks if you can hold 21GB.

models.name: Qwen3-VL 2B
models.blurb: Tiny vision-language model. Handles OCR and screenshots on modest hardware.

models.name: Qwen3-VL 32B
models.blurb: The strongest vision model you can run on one consumer GPU. Excellent charts, documents, and UI understanding.

models.name: Qwen3-VL 235B
models.blurb: The most powerful vision model in the Qwen family. Multi-GPU territory.

models.name: Gemma 4 31B
models.blurb: Google's latest flagship Gemma — multimodal with audio input and thinking mode. Excellent prose quality.

models.name: DeepSeek-V3.1
models.blurb: DeepSeek's flagship MoE. Frontier general capability; server hardware only.

models.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
models.blurb: Mistral's flagship merging instruction-following, reasoning, and coding. Multi-GPU or a very large workstation.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 1.5B (abliterated)
models.blurb: Abliterated build — the learned refusal direction is removed, so it answers prompts the base model would decline. Same weights and quality otherwise.

models.blurb: Abliterated build — the learned refusal direction is removed, so it answers prompts the base model would decline. Same weights and quality otherwise.

models.name: Dolphin Mixtral 8x7B
models.blurb: Uncensored Mixtral tune. Sparse, so it is quicker than 47B suggests — a long-standing favourite for unrestricted chat.

models.name: Dolphin Mixtral 8x22B
models.blurb: The large uncensored Mixtral. Serious hardware, frontier-adjacent quality.

models.name: Dolphin Llama 3 8B
models.blurb: Llama 3 with the refusal training removed. A dependable uncensored 8B.

models.name: Dolphin Llama 3 70B
models.blurb: The large Dolphin Llama 3. Strong general model without the guardrails.

models.name: Dolphin Phi 2.7B
models.blurb: Tiny uncensored model built on Phi-2. Runs on almost anything.

models.name: Llama 2 Uncensored 7B
models.blurb: The original uncensored Llama 2. Dated now, but small and still widely used.

models.name: Llama 2 Uncensored 70B
models.blurb: The large uncensored Llama 2. Superseded by Dolphin Llama 3 in most respects.

models.name: Wizard Vicuna Uncensored 7B
models.blurb: An early uncensored fine-tune. Conversational and light.

models.name: Wizard Vicuna Uncensored 13B
models.blurb: The mid-size Wizard Vicuna. Better prose than the 7B.

models.name: Wizard Vicuna Uncensored 30B
models.blurb: The largest Wizard Vicuna. Short context limits its use today.

models.name: WizardLM Uncensored 13B
models.blurb: Uncensored WizardLM. Follows complex instructions without refusing.

models.name: Nous Hermes 2 10.7B
models.blurb: Nous Research tune on Solar. Strong instruction-following for its size.

models.name: Nous Hermes 2 34B
models.blurb: The large Nous Hermes 2, built on Yi 34B. Excellent general chat.

models.name: OpenHermes 2.5 7B
models.blurb: A well-rounded Mistral tune. Reliable structured output and function calling.

models.name: Mistral OpenOrca 7B
models.blurb: Mistral fine-tuned on the OpenOrca set. Good reasoning for a 7B.

models.name: Neural Chat 7B
models.blurb: Intel's Mistral tune. Solid conversational quality.

models.name: Starling LM 7B
models.blurb: RLAIF-trained 7B that punched well above its weight at release.

models.name: OpenChat 3.5 7B
models.blurb: Trained on mixed-quality data with C-RLFT. Competitive with much larger models at release.

models.name: Zephyr 7B
models.blurb: HuggingFace's DPO-tuned Mistral. Helpful and direct.

models.name: Tulu 3 8B
models.blurb: AI2's fully-open post-training recipe on Llama 3.1. Published data and code.

models.name: Tulu 3 70B
models.blurb: The large fully-open Tulu 3. Reproducible frontier-class post-training.

models.name: Marco-o1 7B
models.blurb: Alibaba's open reasoning model. Chain-of-thought with self-reflection.

models.name: Command R7B
models.blurb: Cohere's smallest RAG model. Grounded generation with citations at 7B.

models.name: Vicuna 7B
models.blurb: A 2023 landmark tune. Historical interest mostly — newer 7Bs beat it comfortably.

models.name: Vicuna 13B
models.blurb: The mid-size Vicuna. Superseded, but still pulled often.

models.name: Vicuna 33B
models.blurb: The largest Vicuna. Short context and dated training.

models.name: Orca Mini 3B
models.blurb: A small Orca-style tune. Light and fast for basic tasks.

models.name: Orca Mini 7B
models.blurb: The 7B Orca Mini. Explanation-style answers.

models.name: Orca Mini 13B
models.blurb: The 13B Orca Mini. Dated but coherent.

models.name: StableLM 2 1.6B
models.blurb: Stability AI's compact multilingual model. Seven languages in under 1GB.

models.name: StableLM 2 12B
models.blurb: The larger StableLM 2. Good multilingual coverage.

models.name: Yi 6B
models.blurb: 01.AI's small bilingual model. Strong Chinese and English.

models.name: Yi 9B
models.blurb: The mid-size Yi. A solid bilingual option.

models.name: Llama 2 7B
models.blurb: Meta's 2023 model. Kept for reproducing old results; Llama 3.2 is better in every way.

models.name: Llama 2 13B
models.blurb: The mid-size Llama 2. Historical.

models.name: Llama 2 70B
models.blurb: The large Llama 2. Superseded by Llama 3.3 70B.

models.name: WizardLM 2 7B
models.blurb: Microsoft's WizardLM 2. Strong multi-turn conversation.

models.name: WizardLM 2 8x22B
models.blurb: The large WizardLM 2 MoE. Was among the strongest open models at release.

models.name: DeepSeek LLM 7B
models.blurb: DeepSeek's first-gen general model. Superseded by their V2/V3 line.

models.name: DeepSeek LLM 67B
models.blurb: The large first-gen DeepSeek. Historical interest.

models.name: SQLCoder 7B
models.blurb: Purpose-built for text-to-SQL. Beats general models on schema-aware query generation.

models.name: SQLCoder 15B
models.blurb: The larger SQLCoder. Handles complex joins and nested queries.

models.name: CodeUp 13B
models.blurb: A Llama-2-based code model. Dated but light.

models.name: Magicoder 7B
models.blurb: Trained on OSS-Instruct synthetic data. Good at idiomatic code.

models.name: BakLLaVA 7B
models.blurb: A Mistral-based vision model. An alternative to LLaVA 7B.
